concepts.title: Definition of trigonometric ratios
concepts.conceptBody:
Trigonometric Ratios

Let a revolving line $\text{OP}$ starts from $\text{OM}$ and revolves into the position $\text{OP}$, thus tracing out the angle MOP.

Draw PM perpendicular to the initial line OM.

In the triangle $\text{MOP},\text{OP}$ is the hypotenuse, $\text{PM}$ is the perpendicular, and $\text{OM}$ is the base. The trigonometrical ratios, or functions, of the angle MOP are defined as follows.

 

$\frac{\text{MP}}{\text{OP}}$, i.e., $\frac{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ Perp}\text{. }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }}{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ Hyp}\text{. }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }}$, is called the Sine of the angle AOP;

$\frac{\text{OM}}{\text{OP}}$, i.e., $\frac{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ Base }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }}{\text{Hyp}.}$, is called the Cosine of the angle AOP;

$\frac{\text{MP}}{\text{OM}}$, i.e., $\frac{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ Perp}\text{. }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }}{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ Base }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }}$, is called the Tangent of the angle AOP;.

$\frac{\text{OM}}{\text{MP}}$, i.e., $\frac{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ Base }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }}{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ Perp}\text{. }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }}$, is called the Cotangent of the angle AOP;

$\frac{\text{OP}}{\text{OM}}$, i.e., $\frac{\text{Hyp}}{\text{Base}}$, is called the Secant of the angle AOP;

$\frac{\text{OP}}{\text{MP}}$, i.e., $\frac{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ Hyp}\text{. }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }}{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ Perp}\text{. }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }}$, is called the Cosecant of the angle AOP;

concepts.title: Signs according to quadrants
concepts.conceptBody:
Sign of Trigonometric Ratios 

As we know that Cartesian plane is divided into 4 quadrants, the sign of trigonometric ratios vary according to the quadrant

You can learn this thing by the following line

\(\begin{array}{ll|ll} \text {"After } & \text { School } & \underline{\text { To }} & \text { College" } \\ \quad \downarrow & \quad \downarrow & ~\downarrow & \quad \downarrow \\ \text { All } & \sin & \tan & \cos \theta \\ \text { T-ratios } & \text { T-ratio } & \text { T-ratios } & \text { T-ratios } \\ & \text { includes } & \text { includes } & \text { includes } \\ & \frac{1}{\sin \theta}=\operatorname{cosec} \theta & \cot \theta & \sec \theta
\end{array}\)

Note : The angle we talk about in trigonometry and all its formulas is in radians and NOT in degrees.

So we convert degree into radians whenever we are given question in degrees.

concepts.title: Complementary Angles
concepts.conceptBody:
Complementary Angles 

Two angles are said to be complementary when their sum is equal to a right angle. Thus any angle $\theta $ and the angle $90-\theta $ are complementary.

concepts.title: Supplementary angles
concepts.conceptBody:
Supplementary angles 

Two angles are said to be supplementary when their sum is equal to two right angles i.e., the supplement of any angle $\theta $ is $180-\theta $.

concepts.title: Allied or Related Angles
concepts.conceptBody:
Allied or Related Angles 

The angles $\frac{1}{2}\text{n}\pi +\theta $ and $\frac{1}{2}\text{n}\pi -\theta $, where is any integer, are known as allied or related angles.

The trigonometric functions of these angles can be expressed as trigonometric functions of $\theta $, with either a plus or a minus sign. The following working rules can be used in determining these functions.

for complimentary angles , T-ratio interchange

$\begin{array}{*{35}{l}} \text{sin}\left( \frac{\pi }{2}-\theta \right)=\text{cos}\theta & {} & \text{cos}\left( \frac{\pi }{2}-\theta \right)=\text{sin}\theta \\ \text{tan}\left( \frac{\pi }{2}-\theta \right)=\text{cot}\theta & , & \text{cot}\left( \frac{\pi }{2}-\theta \right)=\text{tan}\theta \\ \text{cosec}\left( \frac{\pi }{2}-\theta \right)=\text{sec}\theta \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ } & {} & \text{sec}\left( \frac{\pi }{2}-\theta \right)=\text{cosec}\theta \\ \end{array}$

for supplementary angles, T-ratios remain same though value may become negative relative to quadrant

$\begin{array}{*{35}{l}} \text{sin}\left( \pi -\theta \right)=\text{sin}\theta & \text{sin}\left( \pi +\theta \right)=-\text{sin}\theta  \\ \text{cos}\left( \pi -\theta \right)=-\text{cos}\theta & \text{cos}\left( \pi +\theta \right)=-\text{cos}\theta \\ \text{tan}\left( \pi -\theta \right)=-\text{tan}\theta & \text{tan}\left( \pi +\theta \right)=+\text{tan}\theta \\ \end{array}$

Table of formulae for allied angles 

For learning this table use the concept done earlier about quadrants

Note : We put the sign according to T-ratio on the left not on the R.H.S.

Also note T-ratios for $-\theta $

$\begin{array}{*{35}{r}} {} & \text{sin}\left( -\theta \right)=-\text{sin}\theta \\ {} & \text{cos}\left( -\theta \right)=\text{cos}\theta \\ {} & \text{tan}\left( -\theta \right)=-\text{tan}\theta \\ \end{array}$

concepts.title: Basic formulas
concepts.conceptBody:
Basic Formulas 

$ ext{si}{{ ext{n}}^{2}} heta + ext{co}{{ ext{s}}^{2}} heta =1$ 

$1+ ext{ta}{{ ext{n}}^{2}} heta = ext{se}{{ ext{c}}^{2}} heta ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }$ or $ ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ se}{{ ext{c}}^{2}} heta - ext{ta}{{ ext{n}}^{2}} heta =1$

$1+ ext{co}{{ ext{t}}^{2}} heta = ext{cose}{{ ext{c}}^{2}} heta ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }$ or $ ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ cose}{{ ext{c}}^{2}} heta - ext{co}{{ ext{t}}^{2}} heta =1$

Some formulas derived from above results 

  • $ ext{sin} heta =\pm \sqrt{1- ext{co}{{ ext{s}}^{2}} heta }$ or $ ext{cos} heta =\pm \sqrt{1- ext{si}{{ ext{n}}^{2}} heta }$
  • $ ext{sec} heta + ext{tan} heta = rac{1}{ ext{sec} heta - ext{tan} heta }$
  • $ ext{cosec} heta + ext{cot} heta = rac{1}{ ext{cosec} heta - ext{cot} heta }$

Some algebraic formulas useful in problems: 

  • ${{ ext{a}}^{2}}+{{ ext{b}}^{2}}={{( ext{a}+ ext{b})}^{2}}-2 ext{ab}$
  • ${{a}^{3}}+{{b}^{3}}={{(a+b)}^{3}}-3ab\left( a+b ight)$
  • ${{a}^{3}}-{{b}^{3}}={{(a-b)}^{3}}+3ab\left( a-b ight)$
  • $ ext{a}+ ext{b}+ ext{c}=0 ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }\Rightarrow ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }{{ ext{a}}^{3}}+{{ ext{b}}^{3}}+{{ ext{c}}^{3}}=0$

Componendo & dividendo : 

If $ ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ } rac{ ext{p}}{ ext{q}}= rac{ ext{a}}{ ext{b}}$, then by using componendo & dividendo

$ rac{p+q}{p-q}= rac{a+b}{a-b}$

Q) Prove that $\sqrt{ rac{1+ ext{sin} heta }{1- ext{sin} heta }}= ext{sec} heta + ext{tan} heta $ 

Solution : 

L.H.S. $=\sqrt{ rac{1+ ext{sin} heta }{1- ext{sin} heta }}=\sqrt{ rac{1+ ext{sin} heta }{1- ext{sin} heta }\cdot rac{1+ ext{sin} heta }{1+ ext{sin} heta }}$

$=\sqrt{ rac{{{(1+ ext{sin} heta )}^{2}}}{1- ext{si}{{ ext{n}}^{2}} heta }}=\sqrt{ rac{{{(1+ ext{sin} heta )}^{2}}}{ ext{co}{{ ext{s}}^{2}} heta }}= rac{1+ ext{sin} heta }{ ext{cos} heta }$

$= rac{1}{ ext{cos} heta }+ rac{ ext{sin} heta }{ ext{cos} heta }= ext{sec} heta + ext{tan} heta =$ R.H.S.

Q) Show that $\left( 1+ ext{cot}A- ext{cosec}A ight)\left( 1+ ext{tan}A+ ext{sec}A ight)=2$ 

Solution : 

L.H.S. $=\left( 1+ ext{cotA}- ext{cosecA} ight)\left( 1+ ext{tanA}+ ext{secA} ight)$

$=\left( 1+ rac{ ext{cosA}}{ ext{sinA}}- rac{1}{ ext{sinA}} ight)\left( 1+ rac{ ext{sinA}}{ ext{cosA}}+ rac{1}{ ext{cosA}} ight)$

$=\left( rac{ ext{sinA}+ ext{cosA}-1}{ ext{sinA}} ight)\left( rac{ ext{cosA}+ ext{sinA}+1}{ ext{cosA}} ight)$

$= rac{{{( ext{sinA}+ ext{cosA})}^{2}}-{{1}^{2}}}{ ext{sinAcosA}}$

$= rac{ ext{si}{{ ext{n}}^{2}} ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ A}+ ext{co}{{ ext{s}}^{2}} ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ A}+2 ext{sinAcosA}-1}{ ext{sinAcosA}}$

$= rac{2 ext{sinAcosA}}{ ext{sinAcosA}}=2=$ R.H.S.

Q) If $ ext{ta}{{ ext{n}}^{2}} heta =1-{{e}^{2}}$, prove that $ ext{sec} heta + ext{ta}{{ ext{n}}^{3}} heta ext{cosec} heta ={{\left( 2-{{e}^{2}} ight)}^{ rac{3}{2}}}$ 

Solution : 

Given $ ext{ta}{{ ext{n}}^{2}} heta =1-{{ ext{e}}^{2}}$

Now, L.H.S. $= ext{sec} heta + ext{ta}{{ ext{n}}^{3}} heta ext{cosec} heta $ =$ ext{sec} heta \left( 1+ ext{ta}{{ ext{n}}^{3}} heta rac{ ext{cosec} heta }{ ext{sec} heta } ight)$

=$ ext{sec} heta \left( 1+ ext{ta}{{ ext{n}}^{3}} heta \cdot ext{cot} heta ight)$= sec θ (1+tan 2 θ ) $= ext{sec} heta ext{se}{{ ext{c}}^{2}} heta $

$= ext{se}{{ ext{c}}^{3}} heta ={{\left( ext{se}{{ ext{c}}^{2}} heta ight)}^{ rac{3}{2}}}={{\left( 1+ ext{ta}{{ ext{n}}^{2}} heta ight)}^{ rac{3}{2}}}$

$={{\left( 1+1-{{ ext{e}}^{2}} ight)}^{ rac{3}{2}}}={{\left( 2-{{ ext{e}}^{2}} ight)}^{ rac{3}{2}}}$

Q) $ ext{Cosec} heta - ext{sin} heta =m$ and $ ext{sec} heta - ext{cos} heta =n$, eliminate $ heta $. 

Solution : 

Given, $ ext{cosec} heta - ext{sin} heta = ext{m}$ or, $ rac{1}{ ext{sin} heta }- ext{sin} heta = ext{m}$

or, $ ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ } rac{1- ext{si}{{ ext{n}}^{2}} heta }{ ext{sin} heta }= ext{m }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ or, }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ } rac{ ext{co}{{ ext{s}}^{2}} heta }{ ext{sin} heta }= ext{m}$ …(i)

Again $ ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ sec} heta - ext{cos} heta =n$ or, $ ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ } rac{1}{ ext{cos} heta }- ext{cos} heta =n$

or, $ ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ } rac{1- ext{co}{{ ext{s}}^{2}} heta }{ ext{cos} heta }=n ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }$ or, $ ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ } rac{ ext{si}{{ ext{n}}^{2}} heta }{ ext{cos} heta }=n$ …(ii)

From (i), $ ext{sin} heta = rac{ ext{co}{{ ext{s}}^{2}} heta }{m}$

Putting in (ii), we get $ rac{ ext{co}{{ ext{s}}^{4}} heta }{{{ ext{m}}^{2}} ext{cos} heta }= ext{n }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }$ or, $ ext{co}{{ ext{s}}^{3}} heta ={{ ext{m}}^{2}} ext{n}$

$ herefore ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ cos} heta ={{\left( {{m}^{2}}n ight)}^{ rac{1}{3}}}$ or, $ ext{co}{{ ext{s}}^{2}} heta ={{\left( {{m}^{2}}n ight)}^{ rac{2}{3}}}$ …(iv)

From (iii), $ ext{sin} heta = rac{ ext{co}{{ ext{s}}^{2}} heta }{m}= rac{{{\left( {{m}^{2}}n ight)}^{ rac{2}{3}}}}{m}= rac{{{m}^{ rac{4}{3}}}{{n}^{ rac{2}{3}}}}{m}={{m}^{ rac{1}{3}}}{{n}^{ rac{2}{3}}}={{\left( m{{n}^{2}} ight)}^{ rac{1}{3}}}$

$ herefore ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ si}{{ ext{n}}^{2}} heta ={{\left( ext{m}{{ ext{n}}^{2}} ight)}^{ rac{2}{3}}}$ …(v)

adding (iv) and (v), we get

${{\left( {{m}^{2}}n ight)}^{ rac{2}{3}}}+{{\left( m{{n}^{2}} ight)}^{ rac{2}{3}}}= ext{co}{{ ext{s}}^{2}} heta + ext{si}{{ ext{n}}^{2}} heta $

or, $ ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }{{\left( {{m}^{2}}n ight)}^{ rac{2}{3}}}+{{\left( m{{n}^{2}} ight)}^{ rac{2}{3}}}=1$.

Problems based on limits between which $ ext{sin} heta , ext{cos} heta , ext{sec} heta , ext{cosec} heta $, lies or does not lie.

Working Rule : Use the following whichever is applicable.

(i) $-1\le ext{sin} heta \le 1$ or, $\left| ext{sin} heta ight|\le 1$ or, $ ext{si}{{ ext{n}}^{2}} heta \le 1$

(ii) $-1\le ext{cos} heta \le 1$ or, $ ext{co}{{ ext{s}}^{2}} heta \le 1$

(iii) $ ext{sec} heta \le -1$ or, $ ext{sec} heta \ge 1$ i.e. $ ext{se}{{ ext{c}}^{2}} heta \ge 1$

(iv) $ ext{cosec} heta \le 1$ or, $ ext{cosec} heta \ge 1$ i.e. $ ext{cose}{{ ext{c}}^{2}} heta \ge 1$

Q) Is the equation $ ext{se}{{ ext{c}}^{2}} heta = rac{4xy}{{{(x+y)}^{2}}}$ possible for real values of $x$ and $y$ ? 

Solution : 

Given, $ ext{se}{{ ext{c}}^{2}} heta = rac{4xy}{{{(x+y)}^{2}}}$

$ ecause ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ se}{{ ext{c}}^{2}} heta \ge 1 ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ } herefore ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ } rac{4 ext{xy}}{{{( ext{x}+ ext{y})}^{2}}}\ge 1$

or, $ ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }{{(x+y)}^{2}}\le 4xy ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }$ or, $ ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }{{(x+y)}^{2}}-4xy\le 0 ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }$ or, ${{(x-y)}^{2}}\le 0$

But for real values of $ ext{x}$ and $ ext{y},{{( ext{x}- ext{y})}^{2}}<0$

$ herefore ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }{{( ext{x}- ext{y})}^{2}}=0 ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ } herefore ext{x}= ext{y }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }$ Also $ ext{x}+ ext{y} e 0\Rightarrow ext{x} e 0. ext{y} e 0$

$ herefore ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }$ given equation $ ext{se}{{ ext{c}}^{2}} heta = rac{4 ext{xy}}{{{( ext{x}+ ext{y})}^{2}}}$ is possible for real values of $ ext{x}$ and $ ext{y}$ only when $ ext{x}= ext{y}$

and $x e 0$

Q) Show that the equation $ ext{sin} heta =x+ rac{1}{x}$ is impossible if $x$ is real. 

Solution : 

Given $ ext{sin} heta = ext{x}+ rac{1}{ ext{x}}$

$ herefore ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ sin} heta ={{x}^{2}}+ rac{1}{{{x}^{2}}}+2x\cdot rac{1}{x}={{x}^{2}}+ rac{1}{{{x}^{2}}}+2\ge 2$

which is not possible since $ ext{si}{{ ext{n}}^{2}} heta \le 1$

Q) If $ ext{sin} heta + ext{si}{{ ext{n}}^{2}} heta =1$, then prove that $ ext{co}{{ ext{s}}^{12}} heta +3 ext{co}{{ ext{s}}^{10}} heta +3 ext{co}{{ ext{s}}^{8}} heta + ext{co}{{ ext{s}}^{6}} heta -1=0$ 

Solution : 

Given $ ext{sin} heta =1- ext{si}{{ ext{n}}^{2}} heta = ext{co}{{ ext{s}}^{2}} heta$.

L.H.S. $ ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }= ext{Co}{{ ext{s}}^{6}} heta {{\left[ ext{co}{{ ext{s}}^{2}} heta +1 ight]}^{3}}-1$

$ egin{align}  & = ext{si}{{ ext{n}}^{3}} heta {{(1+ ext{sin} heta )}^{3}}-1 \ & ={{\left( ext{sin} heta + ext{si}{{ ext{n}}^{2}} heta ight)}^{3}}-1={{1}^{3}}-1=0 \ \end{align}$

Q) $ ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ If }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }{{t}_{n}}= ext{si}{{ ext{n}}^{n}} heta + ext{co}{{ ext{s}}^{n}} heta ext{, }\!\!~\!\! ext{ then }\!\!~\!\! ext{ } rac{{{t}_{3}}-{{t}_{5}}}{{{t}_{1}}}= rac{{{t}_{5}}-{{t}_{7}}}{{{t}_{3}}}$

Solution : 

L.H.S $=[({{\sin }^{3}} heta +{{\cos }^{3}} heta )-({{\sin }^{5}} heta +{{\cos }^{4}} heta )]\div (\sin heta +\cos heta )$

$ egin{align} & =[{{\sin }^{3}} heta (1-{{\sin }^{2}} heta )+{{\cos }^{3}} heta (1-{{\cos }^{2}} heta )]\div (\sin heta +\cos heta ) \ & ={{\sin }^{2}} heta {{\cos }^{2}} heta [(\sin heta +\cos heta )\div (\sin heta +\cos heta )] \ & ={{\sin }^{2}} heta {{\cos }^{2}} heta \ \end{align}$

the L.H.S. is

$ ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ L} ext{.H} ext{.S} ext{. }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }= rac{{{t}_{3}}-{{t}_{5}}}{{{t}_{1}}}$

now we will solve R.H.S.

R.H.S $= rac{{{t}_{5}}-{{t}_{7}}}{{{t}_{3}}}$

$ egin{align} & = rac{({{\sin }^{5}} heta +{{\cos }^{5}} heta )-({{\sin }^{7}} heta +{{\cos }^{7}} heta )}{({{\sin }^{2}} heta +{{\cos }^{3}} heta )}= rac{{{\sin }^{5}} heta (1-{{\sin }^{2}} heta )+{{\cos }^{5}} heta (1-{{\cos }^{2}} heta )}{{{\sin }^{3}} heta +{{\cos }^{3}} heta } \ & = rac{{{\sin }^{5}} heta {{\cos }^{2}} heta +{{\cos }^{5}} heta {{\sin }^{2}} heta }{{{\sin }^{3}} heta +{{\cos }^{3}} heta }= rac{{{\sin }^{2}} heta {{\cos }^{2}} heta ({{\sin }^{3}} heta +{{\cos }^{3}} heta )}{({{\sin }^{3}} heta +{{\cos }^{3}} heta )} \ & =L.H.S \ \end{align}$

Hence proved

Q) ${{\left( rac{1+ ext{sin} heta - ext{cos} heta }{1+ ext{sin} heta + ext{cos} heta } ight)}^{2}}= rac{1- ext{cos} heta }{1+ ext{cos} heta }$ 

Solution : 

L.H.S $= rac{{{(1+\sin heta )}^{2}}+{{\cos }^{2}} heta -2\cos heta (1+\sin heta )}{{{(1+\sin heta )}^{2}}+{{\cos }^{2}} heta +2\cos heta (1+\sin heta )}$

$ egin{align} & = rac{1+{{\sin }^{2}} heta +{{\cos }^{2}} heta +2\sin heta -2\cos heta (1+\sin heta )}{1+{{\sin }^{2}} heta +{{\cos }^{2}} heta +2\sin heta +2\cos heta (1+\sin heta )} \ & = rac{2(1+\sin heta )-2\cos heta (1+\sin heta )}{2(1+\sin heta )+2\cos heta (1+\sin heta )}= rac{1-\cos heta }{1+\cos heta } \ \end{align}$

We have cancelled $2\left( 1+ ext{sin} heta ight)$ from ${{ ext{N}}^{ ext{r}}}$ and ${{ ext{D}}^{ ext{r}}}$.
